title: Learning Music with Ableton
description: Free interactive guide that teaches music fundamentals directly in the browser — beats, melody, harmony, structure. No prior experience required.
author: Ableton

title: Learning Synths
description: Hands-on browser tutorial covering oscillators, filters, envelopes, and modulation. The fastest path from 'what is a synth' to 'I can program one'.
author: Ableton

title: Andrew Huang — Music Production Essentials
description: One of the best producer-educators on YouTube. Covers sound design, mixing, sampling, and workflow tricks across a huge range of genres.
author: Andrew Huang

title: Dan Worrall — Deep Mixing Concepts
description: Technically rigorous breakdowns of compression, EQ, saturation, and psychoacoustics. The channel that corrects the myths you picked up elsewhere.
author: Dan Worrall

title: Sound on Sound Magazine
description: Long-running pro audio publication. Deep technique articles, gear reviews, and the canonical 'Mix Rescue' series where engineers fix readers' mixes.
author: Sound on Sound

title: Produce Like A Pro
description: Warren Huart's channel — mixing workshops, interviews with legendary producers, and practical tips you can apply in your next session.
author: Warren Huart

title: The Complete Guide to Reaktor
description: Modular synthesis environment reference. If you want to build your own instruments and effects from primitives, this is where you start.
author: Native Instruments

title: Tape Op Magazine
description: Engineer-focused magazine with a DIY/indie slant. Great interviews with working producers about what actually happens in their sessions.
author: Tape Op

title: Wavetable Synthesis Explained
description: Understand how wavetable synths (Serum, Vital, Massive X) actually work — wavetables as 2D position arrays, warp functions, and modulation targets.
author: Various

title: Mixing Secrets for the Small Studio
description: Mike Senior's methodical, reference-grade mixing textbook. Also includes hundreds of free multitracks so you can practice on real songs.
author: Mike Senior
